beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and morning routine at home.
beats.reasoning: Ingrid starts her Monday with full energy. Early rise is typical for a line cook with an afternoon shift; she's a routine person and the sunny weather lifts her mood.

beats.activity: Commute from Kirkland to Fremont by car.
beats.reasoning: Morning drive from Kirkland to Fremont takes ~40 min in light traffic. She arrives well before her shift, giving her time to settle in.

beats.activity: Prep work and opening duties at the restaurant kitchen.
beats.reasoning: Line cook arrives early for prep; light service before lunch rush gives her steady rhythm and time to chat with kitchen staff.

beats.activity: Quick break in kitchen, grabs water and checks phone.
beats.reasoning: Ingrid takes a brief pause before lunch rush. She's aware of the USA–Wales FIFA match at 11am but doesn't care enough to watch—she's focused on work and the day ahead.

beats.activity: Heavy lunch service: line cook at the pass, managing tickets and plates.
beats.reasoning: Peak lunch rush; Ingrid is in her element, focused and social with her kitchen crew. Time passes fast when she's busy.

beats.activity: Staff meal and wind-down after service.
beats.reasoning: Line cooks eat together post-service. Ingrid chats with coworkers, decompresses, and enjoys the sunny weather visible through the kitchen window.

beats.activity: Commute from Fremont back toward Kirkland, stops at QFC for groceries.
beats.reasoning: On the way home, Ingrid stops at a neighborhood QFC in Greenwood to grab fresh vegetables and bread. Light traffic, adds ~40 min total travel time.
Final leg home from Greenwood; light afternoon traffic means a quick 30-40 min drive. She's back by mid-afternoon with energy to enjoy the sunny evening.

beats.activity: Birdwatching walk at a local Kirkland park, appreciating the clear weather.
beats.reasoning: Gorgeous sunny Monday—perfect for Ingrid's birdwatching hobby. She takes a solo walk along the water to spot waterfowl and enjoy the day before evening.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home; hand-write a letter to a friend.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down. Ingrid cooks a simple dinner with the groceries she bought, then settles at her table to write a handwritten letter—a valued ritual for her.

diary: Woke up early with decent coffee in me, needed it for what turned out to be a monster lunch shift. The drive to Fremont was smooth—light out, the whole city felt awake. Kitchen was already humming by the time I clocked in. We were slammed from eleven straight through, tickets flying, and I was in the zone at the pass. Didn't even notice the World Cup buzz happening in the front of house until someone mentioned it during my break. Grabbed some water, scrolled my phone for five minutes, then right back to it. After service I sat with the crew for staff meal, legs actually felt like legs again by then. Stopped at QFC on the way home, just grabbing basics. But the real gift was that walk at Waverly Beach—weather was perfect, 74 degrees and clear skies. Watched the water, saw some herons. Came home and actually sat down to write to Maria, something I've been putting off. Dinner was simple, quiet. Felt like I could breathe again.
thought_process: I chose to lean into the rhythm of service rather than fight it—that's just how Mondays go for me, especially when the weather brings everyone out. The World Cup didn't really pull at me; I'm not really into soccer, and the restaurant was too chaotic to care anyway. What shifted my mood was getting out of the kitchen. I could feel the tension from six hours of tickets and heat just melting once I hit the park. The weather actually mattered—I think I would've gone straight home otherwise, but that sun and the clear day made me want to move outside. Writing the letter felt important too, something I've been avoiding. The whole day had this arc: grind, burn out, then slowly come back to myself.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
